lang.name: English
topbar.lang.label: Language
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Operational overview for profile
dashboard.empty.no_30d: No prints in the last 30 days yet.
dashboard.empty.sizes: No size data yet — print a label that includes ^PW/^LL.
dashboard.empty.items: No items captured yet — print using a template that has a lookup field.
dashboard.empty.consumables: Roll status per printer — coming soon.
history.title: Print history
form.title: New label
form.subtitle: Pick a template, fill in the fields and preview before printing.
form.preview.title: Preview
form.preview.help: Press Preview to render the label via Labelary.
form.no_fields_hint: This template has no editable fields. Press Print to send it as-is.
form.print_overrides.title: Print settings (advanced)
form.print_overrides.hint: Override media type, speed and darkness for this job only. Defaults come from the template; you can change them permanently in Settings → Templates → Edit.
settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: Configure Comandante Zebra by topic. Each section is independent — you can come back to any of them later.
settings.wizard.cta.title: Setup Wizard
settings.wizard.cta.subtitle: Guided walkthrough — connection, printer, first template.
settings.tile.printers.title: Printers
settings.tile.printers.desc: Default printer for all templates and per-template overrides.
settings.tile.templates.title: Templates & fields
settings.tile.templates.desc: Import .zpl files and configure the form fields users fill in to print them.
settings.tile.connections.title: Database connections
settings.tile.connections.desc: Servers used by database fields to pull values from real tables (products, customers…).
wizard.title: Setup Wizard
wizard.subtitle: Get Comandante Zebra ready for everyday printing in a few steps.
wizard.success: You're ready to print. Head to the Print page to try it.
wizard.printer_setup.title: Printer setup
wizard.printer_setup.subtitle: We'll walk you through connecting your Zebra step by step.
wizard.s1.title: Welcome
wizard.s1.intro: This wizard configures your Zebra printer so the app can send labels. We detected you're running on
wizard.s1.hint_windows: On Windows you can use either a printer installed via the Zebra driver (USB or network) or talk directly to a networked Zebra over TCP port 9100.
wizard.s1.hint_unix: On macOS/Linux you can use a CUPS queue (USB or network, managed by the OS) or talk directly to a networked Zebra over TCP port 9100.
wizard.s1.already_configured: A printer is already configured. You can reconfigure it below or jump back to the dashboard.
wizard.s2.title: How is the Zebra connected?
wizard.s2.system.desc_windows: Recommended for USB Zebras. Install the Zebra driver first, then pick the printer here.
wizard.s2.system.desc_unix: Recommended for USB Zebras on Mac/Linux. The printer must already be added to CUPS.
wizard.s2.tcp.title: Network (Ethernet / Wi-Fi)
wizard.s2.tcp.desc: Works on any OS. You'll need the printer's IP address and port 9100 open on your network.
wizard.s2.advanced.title: Advanced
wizard.s2.advanced.desc: Manually enter a target like tcp://host:port, win://Name or cups://queue.
wizard.s3.title: Configuration
wizard.s3.no_printers_windows: No printers detected. Install the Zebra driver from Windows Update or zebra.com, plug the printer in and reload.
wizard.s3.no_printers_unix: No printers detected. Add the printer via CUPS at localhost:631 and reload.
wizard.s3.tcp.hint: Find the IP on the printer's info label (press and hold Feed to print network settings). Standard ZPL port is 9100.
wizard.s3.advanced.label: Raw target
wizard.s3.templates_dir_hint: Folder containing your .zpl files, relative to the app.
wizard.s4.title: Test the connection
wizard.s4.intro: Run a quick reachability check, then send a small test label to make sure everything is wired up correctly.
wizard.s5.title: All set
wizard.s5.footer: You can reopen the wizard anytime, or tweak individual settings under Settings.
printers.title: Printers
printers.subtitle: The default printer is used for any template that doesn't have its own override.
printers.empty.overrides: No per-template overrides — every template uses the default.
templates_list.title: Templates
templates_list.subtitle: Each template is a .zpl file. Configure its fields so the print form knows what to ask for.
templates_list.empty: No templates yet — import your first .zpl to get started.
connections.title: Database connections
connections.subtitle: Servers used by lookup fields. Credentials are stored in secrets.cfg.
connections.col.name: Name
connections.driver.bundled: This build ships with pymssql, which works against any SQL Server using SQL authentication.
connections.driver.win_auth_note: For Windows / AD authentication you'd need pyodbc plus the Microsoft ODBC Driver 18 — not included in this build.
connections.driver.pyodbc_note: Using pyodbc requires the Microsoft ODBC Driver 18 to be installed system-wide on this machine.
connections.driver.pick_one: Pick whichever fits your machine and install it before saving a connection:
profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.subtitle: Each profile is a self-contained directory with its own templates, connections and history.
profiles.restart_required: Switching profiles requires restarting the app.
tmpl.new.title: New template
tmpl.new.subtitle: Paste your ZPL or upload a .zpl file. We'll parse it and let you configure each field.
tmpl.fields.title: Edit template
tmpl.fields.subtitle: Print settings and form fields for this label.
tmpl.fields.section_fields_desc: Decide what users see and which values feed each ZPL field.
tmpl.source.title: ZPL source for
tmpl.source.subtitle: Edit the raw ZPL. Be careful — this is what's sent to the printer.
tmpl.print.section_desc: Per-template overrides for the printer. Leave any control on the default to inherit whatever the template/printer already has.
tmpl.print.speed_hint: Inches per second. 0 = inherit. Typical Zebra range: 1-14.
tmpl.print.darkness_hint: 0-30 for the burn intensity. -1 = inherit. Higher = darker print.
network.title: Network
network.me.title: This instance
network.me.subtitle: How others see you on the local network. Edit and save to broadcast the change.
network.me.pin_hint: Other instances need this 6-digit PIN to pull templates or connections from you.
network.peers.title: Discovered peers
network.peers.subtitle: Other Comandante Zebra instances seen on this LAN via mDNS.
network.pull.title: Browse peer
network.pull.pin_prompt: Enter the 6-digit PIN displayed on that peer's Network page:
network.diag.title: Diagnostics
network.diag.subtitle: If peers don't show up, this is the first place to look.
network.diag.advice.zeroconf_missing: The zeroconf Python package is not installed. Without it, this instance can't see other peers nor announce itself. Install it with `uv sync` (or `pip install zeroconf`) and restart the app.
network.diag.advice.zeroconf_init_failed: Zeroconf failed to start. The most common reason on Windows is that the Bonjour service is not installed. Installing 'Bonjour Print Services' from Apple usually fixes it.
network.diag.advice.publish_failed: We couldn't announce this instance on mDNS. Almost always a firewall blocking UDP port 5353 outbound. On Windows, allow Comandante Zebra (or python.exe) through Windows Defender Firewall — Private network is enough. On macOS, no action is normally needed.
network.diag.advice.no_peers_seen: We're listening but no peers have shown up yet. Possible causes: you're alone on this LAN, your firewall is blocking inbound UDP/5353, or the other instance is on a different subnet (mDNS is link-local, it doesn't cross routers).
network.diag.advice.no_lan_ip: Couldn't detect a LAN IP for this machine. Check your network connection — peers can't reach you over a loopback address.
network.diag.advice.not_publishing: Browser is up but we're not announcing ourselves yet. This usually means the desktop wrapper hasn't told us which port Flask is on; restart the app or run via `desktop.py`.
network.firewall.open_hint: Adds inbound + outbound rules for UDP 5353 (mDNS) so peers can find you. On Windows you'll get a UAC prompt — accept it. On macOS and Linux, run the command below by hand.
update.dialog.title: Update available
tools.title: Tools
tools.subtitle: One-shot utilities sent to your default printer. Each button maps to a single ZPL command — handy for diagnostics, calibration and quick tests.
tools.no_target_hint: Set a default printer in Settings → Printers before using Tools.
tools.confirm.generic: This will affect the printer. Continue?
tools.group.diagnostics.desc: Have the printer print its own status — model, dpi, network, sensor profile.
tools.group.calibration.desc: Re-train the printer's media sensors and detect the current label length.
tools.group.maintenance.desc: Operational helpers: feed, save settings to flash, reboot.
tools.group.test.desc: Print a small label to confirm the pipeline works end-to-end.
tools.config_label.title: Print Configuration Label
tools.config_label.desc: The printer prints its full config: dpi, firmware, dimensions, network, sensor settings. Best way to confirm the actual print resolution (8 DPMM = 203 dpi, 12 DPMM = 300 dpi).
tools.sensor_profile.title: Print Sensor Profile
tools.sensor_profile.desc: Visual sensor profile useful when the printer mis-detects the start of a label.
tools.network_config.title: Print Network Config
tools.network_config.desc: Network settings: IP, MAC, gateway, port (only meaningful on Ethernet/Wi-Fi printers).
tools.calibrate_media.title: Calibrate Media Sensor
tools.calibrate_media.desc: Re-runs media sensor calibration and saves the result to flash. Use after changing label stock.
tools.detect_label_length.title: Detect Label Length
tools.detect_label_length.desc: Asks the printer to advance and measure label length. Settings persist in volatile memory unless saved.
tools.form_feed.title: Form Feed
tools.form_feed.desc: Advance one blank label — same as pressing the Feed button.
tools.save_settings.title: Save Settings to Flash
tools.save_settings.desc: Persist any pending volatile settings (calibration, length, etc.) to non-volatile memory.
tools.reset_printer.title: Reset Printer
tools.reset_printer.desc: Software reboot of the printer. Asks for confirmation. Pending jobs are lost.
tools.test_label.title: Print Test Label
tools.test_label.desc: Sends a small "Comandante Zebra OK" label using the printer's persistent dimensions. Ideal as a smoke test.
versions.dialog.title: Version history
versions.dialog.empty: No previous versions saved yet — the first edit will create one.
versions.dialog.confirm_restore: Replace the current template with this version? The current state will be saved as a new version first, so you can undo this.
versions.dialog.compare_hint: Tick up to two versions to compare, or one to compare with current
